paper_title: Systematic study of the diatoms (Bacillariophyta) of Joal-Fadiouth Lagoon (Senegal)

methods.description: Process for collecting phytoplankton and phytobenthos samples from Joal-Fadiouth Lagoon, fixing them, cleaning diatom frustules, preparing permanent slides for microscopy, and arranging the diatom list systematically.

steps.description: Six samples (phytoplankton and phytobenthos) were collected (March-June 2013)
steps.searchable_summary: Collect biological samples (phytoplankton, phytobenthos) from a specific location during a specific period.
Fix biological samples using formalin solution.

steps.description: Sub-samples underwent a treatment of cleaning of the frustules by combination of calcination to chemical attack of the organic matter,
steps.searchable_summary: Clean diatom frustules from sub-samples using calcination and chemical attack.

steps.description: before being mounted with Canada Balsam to prepare permanent slides for microscopy.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare permanent microscopy slides by mounting cleaned frustules with Canada Balsam.

steps.description: The systematic arrangement of the diatoms list was made by according to [6].
steps.searchable_summary: Arrange the systematic list of identified diatoms based on a specified reference/taxonomy.
